title: Our Lady as Mediatrix and Co-Redemptrix — A Patristic and Doctoral Library

eras.title: Preamble — Definitions and Distinctions
eras.summary: merited by Christ are distributed to the Mystical Body. Subordinate to and dependent on the unique mediation of Christ (1 Tim 2:5). She mediates not as a parallel source but as the maternal instrument willed by God.

eras.title: Part I — Old Testament Types
eras.summary: The Fathers and Doctors read the Old Testament Christologically, and within that Christological reading they consistently identified Marian types: real persons, objects, and events that prefigure her unique cooperation in redemption and her maternal mediation.

eras.title: Part II — Apostolic and Sub-Apostolic (1st-2nd c.)
Part III — Greek Patristic Era (3rd-5th c.)
Part IV — Late Patristic / Early Byzantine (6th-8th c.)
